Gap Layer: Client lifecycle, onboarding, delivery handoff, health, retention, expansion, and proof capture layer.
Gap Impact: Client operators cannot reliably answer who owns intent, what is current, what must happen next, which evidence is authoritative, or how completion is proven.
Root Cause: Domain knowledge exists mainly as DOCX drafts, so it lacks controlled markdown registries, protocols, owners, validation gates, and runtime memory.
Required Solution: Promote intent into the authoritative Client OS layer with source IDs, owner, governance rule, dependency links, validation gate, dashboard signal, memory update, and version path.
Required Protocols: Client Intent protocol: trigger -> source evidence -> owner review -> approval/risk check -> execution or handoff -> validation -> memory/version update.
Required Registries: CLIENT_MASTER_REGISTRY plus Agency Global Source Registry.
Required Governance: Client OS Owner / Client Success Lead approves ownership, risk class, cadence, and escalation route.
Required Ownership: Client OS Owner / Client Success Lead
Required Orchestration: Route through Client orchestration and the agency dependency graph: upstream Sales, Offer, Legal, Finance, Branding, Operations; downstream Operations, Finance, Management, Retention, Expansion, Referrals, Case studies.
Required Validation: Entry criteria, source evidence check, owner sign-off, handoff acceptance, and completion gate.
Required Observability: Dashboard status, freshness date, owner review cadence, risk indicator, and gap closure metric.
Required Evolution: Decision log entry, version log entry, quarterly review, and source registry state update.
Required Dependencies: Upstream: Sales, Offer, Legal, Finance, Branding, Operations. Downstream: Operations, Finance, Management, Retention, Expansion, Referrals, Case studies.
Required Sequencing: 1. Confirm source inventory; 2. create canonical markdown; 3. assign owner/RACI; 4. register dependencies and handoffs; 5. define validation and dashboard signals; 6. approve, version, and schedule evolution review.
Completion Criteria: Client Intent is complete when the markdown exists at the recommended placement, has an owner and cadence, references source IDs, is registered in the local and agency registry, defines validation and observability, and has a version/evolution log entry.
